part.text:
---
## Goal
- Add `controlCtx context.Context` parameter to `handleProxy` functions across the repository

## Constraints & Preferences
- The control context should use the existing `ctx` parameter value as default
- Must be applied everywhere handleProxy is defined or called
- Project must typecheck after changes

## Progress
### Done
- Identified three locations with handleProxy: tcp.go line 30 (handleProxy func), handler.go line 67 (Worker.handleProxy method), tcp_test.go lines 91 and 169 (test calls)
- Queried references for both handleProxy variants
- Verified function signatures via ago_view

### In Progress
- Attempted add_param on tcp.go handleProxy - rejected because function used as value at line 18:53
- Need manual inspection of tcp_test.go to update test call sites
- Worker.handleProxy may require similar approach (method vs standalone func)

### Blocked
- None identified yet; add_param rejection indicates need for manual editing workflow

## Key Decisions
- Function handlesProxy in proxy/tcp package uses ctx as first parameter and must accept controlCtx additionally
- Test call at line 91 passes context.Background() - may need to pass controlCtx context variable as separate argument
- Rejection by add_param suggests some handleProxy references capture the function as a value rather than call it directly

## Next Steps
- Inspect tcp.go to determine where handleProxy is used as returned or captured value (line 18:53)
- Update test file call at line 91 to pass controlCtx appropriately
- Check if any callers of Worker.handleProxy need controlCtx
- After all changes, run typecheck to validate

## Critical Context
- Error message: "function referenced as a value; add_param cannot repair those sites" for handleProxy in proxy/tcp package
- Reference at line 18:53 in tcp.go shows function used as value alongside definition at line 30:6
- Test call line 91 in tcp_test.go currently passes context.Background() as first positional argument
- Control context semantics appear to be separate from request context (ctx parameter)

## Relevant Files
- /boundary/internal/daemon/worker/proxy/tcp/tcp.go: handleProxy definition and caller usage
- /boundary/internal/daemon/worker/handler.go: Worker.handleProxy method 
- /boundary/internal/daemon/worker/proxy/tcp/tcp_test.go: test function calls to handleProxy
